title: Catalog Verification TODO
description: One entry per pattern and per framework. Tracks per-aspect verification status so agents (or contributors) can work through the list item-by-item. Aspect values: 'todo' (not yet checked), 'pass' (verified correct), 'fail' (verified wrong, needs fix), 'na' (does not apply to this entry). Top-level 'verified' is true only when every applicable aspect is pass.

patterns.intent_one_sentence: intent reads as exactly one sentence under 35 words
patterns.context_clear: context is plain English a developer new to the area can follow
patterns.problem_clear: problem clearly states what fails without this pattern
patterns.solution_clear: solution describes the mechanism without recourse to undefined jargon
patterns.constrains_is_restriction: constrains slot reads as a restriction (forbids/must/only) not a benefit
patterns.example_scenario_present: example_scenario field is populated with a plain-English narrative
patterns.diagram_present: diagram.mermaid renders to a meaningful sequence/flow/class/state diagram
patterns.applicability_present: applicability.use_when and do_not_use_when bullets are populated
patterns.variants_present: variants[] populated where the pattern has known implementation shapes
patterns.known_uses_live: every known_uses URL is alive and points to the named system
patterns.no_jargon_undefined: any acronym used in prose is defined in glossary.json or expanded in-place
patterns.edges_correct: every related[] edge points at a real pattern; symmetric/inverse pairs balance

frameworks.vendor_correct: vendor field names the actual maintainer
frameworks.language_correct: language field reflects the framework's primary languages
frameworks.coverage_cells_accurate: every coverage cell (fully/limited/none) reflects current docs
frameworks.no_missing_pattern_support: patterns the framework clearly supports are not omitted from coverage
frameworks.last_analyzed_within_quarter: last_analyzed is within the last 90 days

notes.text: Source-verified against https://haystack.deepset.ai/ (HTTP 200), https://docs.haystack.deepset.ai/docs/agent, https://docs.haystack.deepset.ai/docs/mcptool, https://haystack.deepset.ai/integrations/mcp. Vendor deepset (deepset GmbH) confirmed. Python confirmed (pip install haystack-ai). Coverage cells INACCURATE - several "limited" ratings should be "fully": (1) tool-use rated "limited" but Haystack 2.x has first-class Tool / ComponentTool / Toolset and ToolInvoker primitives - "fully" idiomatic. (2) react rated "limited" but the Agent component IS a loop-based ReAct implementation with tool selection, exit conditions, and streaming callbacks - "fully". (3) step-budget rated "limited" but max_agent_steps is a documented first-class Agent parameter - "fully". (4) structured-output rated "limited" is defensible since state_schema is more stateful than typical structured output. MISSING patterns: "mcp" (Haystack has a dedicated MCP integration - MCPTool + Hayhooks-as-MCP-server, both first-class - "fully" capability completely absent from coverage), "streaming" (streaming_callback on Agent), "memory" (cross-session memory via state_schema/conversational memory components). Strongest miss: mcp.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://docs.langchain.com/ and https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/. url_live: 200, content names LangChain. Vendor LangChain Inc. matches site footer. Languages Python+TypeScript confirmed (Go/Java SDKs are LangSmith client-only, not the framework). Cells likely needing correction: skill-library currently 'limited' but LangChain has no first-class skill-library primitive (deepagents is closer); should consider 'none' or keep 'limited' if interpreting prompt templates as skills. supervisor 'limited' is correct (delegated to langgraph-supervisor). handoff 'limited' is correct (delegated to langgraph). approval-queue 'limited' is correct (interrupt is a langgraph primitive, not langchain core). citation-streaming 'limited' is reasonable (langchain has streaming but no first-class citation type). context-window-packing 'limited' is reasonable (no first-class compactor in langchain v1). chain-of-thought 'limited' and self-refine 'limited' are reasonable (these are prompting patterns, not langchain primitives). Borderline: contextual-retrieval is 'limited' but LangChain has Anthropic ContextualEmbeddings recipe in docs - keep limited (recipe-level). No obvious missing patterns found beyond what's covered.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://temporal.io/. url_live: 200. Vendor 'Temporal Technologies' confirmed. Languages confirmed via SDK selector on landing page: Go, Java, Python, TypeScript, .NET (C#), PHP, Ruby. Coverage cells all 'fully' and source-verified: agent-resumption (durable execution - 'workflows automatically capture state at every step, and in the event of failure, can pick up exactly where they left off'), scheduled-agent (workflow.sleep + Schedules API for cron), event-driven-agent (signals + task queues), compensating-action (Saga pattern - 'what if Saga was simply a try...catch?'), step-budget (activity retries with timeouts, retry policies). Missing patterns to add: 'agent-resumption' is covered but 'session-isolation' should be added (per-workflow-id isolation), 'replan-on-failure' (Temporal supports continueAsNew for replan-style behavior), 'tool-use' (Activities are conceptually tools - first-class), 'fallback-chain' (retry policies + activity options). Strongest miss: tool-use and session-isolation since both are foundational primitives.
